_about.title: Doctrine-hardening — 7 ratified improvements to the agent/governance doctrine, sliced file-disjoint
_about.serves: task #45 (planning-harden host: gate this DESIGN -> fan out file-disjoint slices -> negative-control the new CODE -> commit). This file IS the design: both planes, a coverage-clean file-disjoint slicing, the canonical-source-vs-reference ownership map, the collision map, and the PREP/reference DAG. The load-bearing #4/#5 CODE design is returned in the handoff (and summarized under _code_design).
_about.supersedes: an earlier uncommitted draft of this same file (static customStates/slots REGISTRY + fail-closed /site loader). REWRITTEN to the brief's refined wording: #5 cross-checks the descriptor against SOURCE USAGE (internals.states usage in the .ts + the CSS :state()/:has([slot]) sets), NOT a hand-maintained static registry; #4 is a STATIC dead-slot/role-name guard over site/, NOT a fleet schema-sweep. The source-usage design checks the descriptor against where the fact ACTUALLY lives (the .ts/.css), needs no new registry convention, and requires NO button.ts change.
_about.domain_choice: technical-architecture — the doctrine is a SYSTEM of artifacts (agent role files . skills . process/ADR docs . governance code); the slices are its modules, the 7 improvements its capabilities. Not the visual `components` domain.
_about.method: decomposition, both planes. OUTSIDE-IN = the doctrine artifacts grouped (agent docs . skills . docs . code) down to the single OWNING FILE per slice. INSIDE-OUT = the 7 improvements decomposed into capability-actions, each split into its CANONICAL-owner action and its REFERENCE actions (repair-the-owner: one fact, one home, pointers elsewhere). `hosts` crosses them — it is exactly the ownership map.
_about.slicing_principle: FILE-DISJOINT by OWNING FILE, not by improvement. Improvements collide on files (#1 and #2 both touch orchestration-lead.md; #4 and #5 both touch authoring-components), so a per-improvement fan-out would race. Each leaf slice = one file (or one tightly-coupled file group) owned by ONE worker that applies EVERY improvement touching it. The fan-out is collision-free in a SINGLE tree with NO integration slice (no two slices write the same file) — which is itself improvement #2's pattern, dog-fooded here.
_about.cross_ref_pin: the serial PREP for this very fan-out: the cross-cutting rule WORDING + canonical home is fixed here so the parallel writers (and the reference slices) do not diverge. Canonical homes: #1a self-contained directive = orchestration-lead.md; #1b drain-inbox = handoff-contract.md; #1c committed-tree-source-of-truth = orchestration-lead.md; #2 fan-out execution model = orchestration-design; #2 file-disjoint slicing + serial PREP = decomposing-systems; #3a gate-then-commit = handoff-contract.md; #3b NC-anchor discipline = decomposing-systems; #3c generator/critic = component-reviewer.md; #4 contract-change migration = process.md; #5 source-scan trip-wire = component-descriptor.ts; #6 ADR-Decision->action = decomposing-systems; #7 foreseen-amendment = adr/README.md. Reference slices CITE these homes, never restate the fact.

_build_legend.now: buildable at the current tree (doc edit, or CODE on the existing descriptor module)
_build_legend.code-nc: governance CODE that ships WITH a negative-control proving it is non-vacuous (improvement #3b)
_build_legend.ref: a reference slice — points at a canonical home's settled anchor; must not restate the fact
_build_legend.judgment-gate: verified by a doc-authoring harness or a reviewer, not only by npm gates

nodes.label: orchestration-lead.md — self-contained authoritative directives (#1a) + committed-tree-is-source-of-truth / never re-dispatch a stood-down seat (#1c) + same-tree disjoint fan-out is the default, worktrees the fallback (#2 ref)
nodes.dep: s5 (cites the orchestration-design fan-out home for #2)
nodes.acceptance: Priority 1 restates: every dispatch directive is self-contained + authoritative (restate the decisions so out-of-order delivery is harmless; never send an incremental delta a lagging worker applies to a stale base); a new line states the committed tree is the source of truth — once an artifact is gated, committed, and its seat stood down, a later change is a NEW commit/ADR, never an in-place re-edit, and a stood-down seat is never re-dispatched to edit a committed artifact; the 'worktree isolation' sentence is repointed to 'same-tree disjoint fan-out by default (see orchestration-design); worktrees only when slices mutate overlapping files'.

nodes.label: handoff-contract.md — drain the full inbox before composing a handoff (#1b) + gate and commit are SEPARATE steps: read the gate output, THEN commit; never && a commit onto a test run (#3a)
nodes.acceptance: a new 'Before you hand back' precondition: DRAIN your full inbox first — never compose a handoff one message behind (re-asking an answered question, re-editing an already-committed artifact, or retracting a finding a newer commit fixed are the failure modes). A new clause under Tests/checks run: a green gate is NOT a landed change — read the gate output, then commit as a SEPARATE step; never chain a commit onto a test run with && (a regression rides in if the gate is not actually read).
nodes.gate: all agent bodies already cite this path -> the rule propagates by pointer (no agent-body edits needed)

nodes.label: component-reviewer.md — reinforce the generator/critic split: the independent critic hunts the vacuous gate / the missing negative control (#3c)
nodes.dep: (none — self-canonical; reinforces the existing README generator/critic invariant, which is untouched)
nodes.acceptance: the adversarial-stance section names the specific independent-critic wins from the build: a geometry asserted in source but not measured in a real engine, a [density]/NC smoke that is vacuous, and a gate whose negative control never bit — the reviewer's job is to find the inert realization behind a green surface, structurally separate from the builder.

nodes.label: README.md — repoint the Context-isolation invariant (worktrees -> fallback, #2 ref) + add/repoint the committed-tree invariant (#1c ref)
nodes.dep: s1 (the committed-tree rule's home) + s5 (the fan-out model's home)
nodes.acceptance: the 'Context isolation' invariant line stops asserting worktrees as the default for parallel build dispatches — it states the disjoint same-tree fan-out as default (pointing at orchestration-design) with worktrees as the overlap-only fallback; a new invariant bullet names the committed-tree-is-source-of-truth rule, pointing at orchestration-lead.md. Reference-only: it restates no fact, it points.

nodes.label: orchestration-design — codify the disjoint same-tree fan-out EXECUTION model + demote worktrees (#2 canonical)
nodes.acceptance: best-practices.md gains the same-tree fan-out pattern: dispatch file- AND import-disjoint slices CONCURRENTLY in ONE tree; each worker SELF-GATES only its own path (`npm run check 2>&1 | grep <own-path>` empty + its targeted test) and the HOST runs the authoritative whole-tree gate + the negative controls at the WAVE BOUNDARY; worktree isolation is demoted to 'only when slices mutate overlapping files'. SKILL.md Design step references it. Pairs with decomposing-systems (the slicing side).
nodes.gate: python3 .claude/skills/authoring-skills/scripts/harness_checks.py skill .claude/skills/orchestration-design/SKILL.md -> exit 0 ; python3 .claude/skills/authoring-references/scripts/harness_checks.py reference .claude/skills/orchestration-design/references/best-practices.md -> exit 0

nodes.label: decomposing-systems — file-disjoint slicing + the serial PREP slice (#2 canonical, slicing side) + the negative-control-anchor discipline (#3b canonical) + ADR-Decision->action completeness review step + coverage_check scope note (#6)
nodes.acceptance: best-practices.md gains: (a) the DISJOINTNESS TEST for a parallel fan-out — slice by OWNING FILE so each file has exactly one writer, defer every barrel/shared-file edit to ONE serial integration slice, and run a serial PREP slice that pins shared interfaces/cross-refs before a wide fan-out (the B0/wave-2-prep pattern); (b) the NC-ANCHOR discipline — anchor a negative-control mutation on a UNIQUE code token (a literal that also appears in a comment leaves the code untouched -> a false 'the test did not bite'), CONFIRM the mutation actually applied (grep) before concluding, and treat an unexpected NC result as 'my probe is wrong' FIRST, not 'the test is vacuous'; (c) a documented review step — every ratified or anticipated ADR Decision must map to a decomp action/node before fan-out. SKILL.md + the coverage_check.py docstring note the SCOPE: coverage_check verifies the two PLANES cross-check (node<->action), NOT real-world completeness (an ADR Decision with no decomp action passes coverage yet would not get built).
nodes.gate: python3 .claude/skills/authoring-skills/scripts/harness_checks.py skill .claude/skills/decomposing-systems/SKILL.md -> exit 0 ; coverage_check.py still exits 0 on the existing decomps (docstring-only change)

nodes.label: authoring-components — document the broadened (source-scan) descriptor trip-wire (#5 ref) + the contract-change migration trigger on a slot/role/prop rename (#4 ref)
nodes.dep: s10 (the trip-wire exists) + s8-process (the migration step's home)
nodes.acceptance: the Descriptor step (6) + the Validation loop / DoD note that customStates and slots are now trip-wired against the component SOURCE (the .ts internals.states usage + the .css :state()/:has([slot]) sets), so the descriptor must declare them truthfully; and that renaming a slot/role/prop triggers the contract-change MIGRATION step (process.md). Reference-only: points at the trip-wire (component-descriptor.ts) and the migration step (process.md), restating neither.

nodes.label: process.md — the contract-change MIGRATION step (#4 canonical) + reinforce the NC-anchor discipline in the gate doctrine (#3b ref)
nodes.dep: (none — the migration step's canonical home; the coherence-process doc owns 'loops that repair the source artifact')
nodes.acceptance: a new CONTRACT-CHANGE MIGRATION step under the trip-wire / governing-model section: when a slot/role/prop NAME changes, grep the WHOLE repo for ALL spellings of the old name — BOTH the HTML `slot="x"` form AND the JS `setAttribute('slot','x')` form (and the symmetric `data-role`/prop spellings) — across packages AND site/ before the rename is done; a slot rename that migrated the CSS + one demo but missed another call site is the named failure. A one-line pointer reinforces the NC-anchor discipline (canonical in decomposing-systems) as the gate doctrine's verification rule.
nodes.gate: prose doc — graded against the orchestration rubric (no mechanical harness); whole-tree gates stay green

nodes.label: adr/README.md — the foreseen-amendment ADR pattern (#7 canonical)
nodes.dep: (none — independent; exemplar already exists in ADR-0008's append-only `## Amendment` section)
nodes.acceptance: the Status-lifecycle section distinguishes a FORESEEN AMENDMENT (an append-only `## Amendment` section recording the follow-through of a decision the ADR already anticipated — e.g. ADR-0008's tok-states amendment) from a SUPERSESSION (a REVERSAL of the decision -> a new ADR that supersedes). A foreseen amendment stays in the original ADR (append-only); a reversal never edits the accepted decision's substance.

nodes.label: #5 broaden the descriptor trip-wire to SOURCE USAGE — compareDescriptorToSource over the component .ts/.css (component-descriptor.ts)
nodes.dep: (none — extends the existing trip-wire family; NO button.ts change)
nodes.acceptance: component-descriptor.ts gains (1) a READER FIX: addField captures a bare-scalar sequence item (e.g. customStates `- ready`) under a sentinel key (today `- ready` parses to an EMPTY map — its value is dropped; confirmed); plus a `scalarSeq(desc, field)` helper. (2) Extractors: `collectUsedStates(ts, css)` (union of `internals.states?.(add|delete|toggle|replace|has)('X')` in the .ts AND `:state(X)` in the .css), `collectStyledSlots(css)` (`[slot='X']`), `collectStyledRoles(css)` (`[data-role='X']`). (3) `compareDescriptorToSource(desc, {ts, css})` returning SOURCE_DRIFT_CODES = ['STATE_UNDOCUMENTED','STATE_UNUSED','SLOT_UNDOCUMENTED']: used-states must EQUAL descriptor.customStates (both directions); every CSS-styled slot must be IN descriptor.slots (one-directional — `label` is a documented default cell the CSS never selects, the one asymmetry, documented in the fn comment like enumMembersMatch). The schema (validateComponentDescriptor) is unchanged; this is the trip-wire (drift) family, like compareDescriptorToProps.
nodes.gate: npm run check -> 0 ; npm test (component-descriptor + button-descriptor suites) green

nodes.label: #5 PROOF — fleet/synthetic source-drift tests + per-code negative controls + wire the real button (component-descriptor-sourcewire.test.ts NEW + button-descriptor.test.ts EDIT)
nodes.acceptance: a fleet test (synthetic ts/css strings, mirroring component-descriptor-driftwire.test.ts) proves a faithful descriptor is 0-drift (anti-vacuous: assert the extractors actually found ready/leading/trailing/icon/caret first), then a per-code NC: a `:state(busy)` in the css with no `busy` in customStates -> STATE_UNDOCUMENTED; a customStates `gone` used nowhere -> STATE_UNUSED; a `[slot='badge']` in the css absent from slots -> SLOT_UNDOCUMENTED. Each NC anchors on a UNIQUE token (busy/gone/badge — NOT `ready`, which also appears in comments) and the test confirms the targeted code fires (#3b). button-descriptor.test.ts reads the REAL button.ts + button.css + button.md and asserts compareDescriptorToSource == [] (the ready/leading/trailing/icon/caret truth).
nodes.gate: npm test -> the new + edited suites green; each NC yields EXACTLY its targeted SOURCE_DRIFT code

nodes.label: #4 the /site dead-slot/role-name GUARD — a STATIC vitest probe over site/, reusing the canonical parser + extractors (site-canon.test.ts NEW)
nodes.acceptance: a static test (NOT a render smoke — the failure is a stale NAME; jsdom does not evaluate the :has() grid so a jsdom smoke would not catch the visual bug, and a browser smoke is specimen-coupled + costly). Canonical slot vocab = the UNION of every {name}.md descriptor `slots[].name` (includes `label`); canonical role vocab = the UNION of `collectStyledRoles({name}.css)`. The guard scans every site/**/*.{ts,html} (via node:fs, process.cwd()-relative — the proven button-descriptor.test.ts pattern) for slot/role tokens in BOTH spellings: `slot="X"` / `slot='X'` AND `setAttribute('slot','X')`, likewise `data-role`. Every X must be a canonical name. Anti-vacuous: assert the scan found >=1 real slot token (leading/trailing) before asserting zero violations. NC: a synthetic source string carrying `slot="icn"` (a unique dead token) -> the guard flags it (unit-level), so the check is proven non-vacuous without mutating committed site files.
nodes.gate: npm test -> the guard green over real site/ ; the synthetic dead-name NC flags exactly one violation

actions.label: every orchestrator directive is SELF-CONTAINED + authoritative — restate the decisions so out-of-order delivery is harmless; never send an incremental delta a lagging worker applies to a stale base
a worker DRAINS its full inbox before composing a handoff — never one message behind (re-asking an answered question, re-editing a committed artifact, retracting a finding a newer commit fixed)

the committed tree is the SOURCE OF TRUTH — a gated+committed, stood-down artifact is never re-edited in place (a later change is a new commit/ADR), and a stood-down seat is never re-dispatched to edit it
the README invariant list names the committed-tree rule, pointing at orchestration-lead.md (no restated fact)

the disjoint SAME-TREE fan-out execution model: dispatch file+import-disjoint slices concurrently in ONE tree; each worker self-gates its own path; the HOST runs the whole-tree gate + NCs at the wave boundary; worktrees only on file overlap

the decomposition-side DISJOINTNESS TEST: slice by owning file (single writer per file), defer shared/barrel edits to ONE serial integration slice, and run a serial PREP slice that pins shared interfaces before a wide fan-out
the dispatch rule references the same-tree fan-out model; worktrees are the fallback, not the default
the README Context-isolation invariant is repointed: same-tree disjoint fan-out default, worktrees overlap-only
GATE AND COMMIT ARE SEPARATE STEPS — a green gate is not a landed change; read the gate output, then commit; never && a commit onto a test run
NEGATIVE-CONTROL ANCHOR DISCIPLINE — anchor on a UNIQUE code token, confirm the mutation applied (grep) before concluding, treat an unexpected NC as 'my probe is wrong' first
the gate doctrine reinforces the NC-anchor discipline (pointing at decomposing-systems)
reinforce generator/critic — the independent critic hunts the inert realization behind a green surface (vacuous smoke, NC that never bit), structurally separate from the maker

CONTRACT-CHANGE MIGRATION step: on a slot/role/prop rename, grep the WHOLE repo (packages AND site/) for ALL spellings of the old name — the HTML `slot="x"` AND the JS `setAttribute('slot','x')` forms both

the /site GUARD: a static probe fails on a dead slot/role name anywhere in site/ — canonical names sourced from the descriptors (slots) + the component CSS (roles); non-vacuous via a synthetic dead-name NC
authoring-components documents that a slot/role/prop rename triggers the migration step

broaden the descriptor trip-wire to SOURCE USAGE: compareDescriptorToSource cross-checks customStates vs internals.states-usage (.ts) + :state() (.css), and slots vs the CSS :has([slot]) set — new SOURCE_DRIFT codes + the bare-scalar reader fix
the source-drift trip-wire is proven non-vacuous (fleet synthetic + per-code NC on unique tokens) and wired on the real button
authoring-components documents the broadened (source-scan) trip-wire: declare customStates/slots truthfully — they are checked against the .ts/.css
decomp COMPLETENESS review step: every ratified/anticipated ADR Decision maps to a decomp action/node before fan-out
document coverage_check's SCOPE: it verifies the two planes cross-check, NOT real-world completeness (a Decision with no action passes coverage yet would not get built)
the FORESEEN-AMENDMENT ADR pattern: an append-only `## Amendment` follow-through of a decision the ADR anticipated, distinct from a SUPERSESSION (a reversal -> a new ADR)

_ownership_map._rule: repair-the-owner: each FACT has exactly one canonical home; every other touch is a pointer (reference-by-name/ID). A reference slice points at the owner's settled anchor, never restates the fact.

improvement_1.drain_inbox_before_handoff: CANON s2 handoff-contract.md (propagates to every seat by the pointer each agent body already carries — no execution-lead/planning-lead edits needed)
improvement_1.committed_tree_source_of_truth: CANON s1 orchestration-lead.md; REF s4 README.md invariant list

improvement_2.file_disjoint_slicing_plus_serial_PREP: CANON s6 decomposing-systems (the decomposition side — adjacent, not a duplicate)

improvement_3.nc_anchor_discipline: CANON s6 decomposing-systems; also carried into execution-lead via its decomposing-systems skills: preload (no body edit); REF s8-process gate doctrine
improvement_3.generator_critic: CANON s3 component-reviewer.md (reinforces the existing untouched README invariant)

references: s7-authcomp authoring-components documents the broadened trip-wire

_collision_map._purpose: files touched by >1 improvement — the reason slicing is by-file, not by-improvement. Each file has EXACTLY ONE owning slice (single writer); no two slices write the same file, so the fan-out needs NO integration slice.
_collision_map._no_shared_file: no slice writes a barrel/config/shared file. (If the host instead places the #4 guard UNDER site/, that adds ONE shared edit — vitest.config.ts `include` += 'site/**/*.test.ts' — which becomes the lone serial integration slice. The chosen Option-1 placement avoids it.)

_prep_dag._note: improvement #2's serial-PREP pattern, applied to THIS fan-out: pin the cross-ref wording first (_about.cross_ref_pin), then canonical homes land before the slices that point at them.
_prep_dag.PREP_serial: fix the cross-cutting rule wording + canonical home (_about.cross_ref_pin) so parallel writers and reference slices do not diverge — a coordination pin, no file edit
_prep_dag.host_gate_at_each_wave_boundary: wave 1+2 docs: harness_checks agent/skill/reference per authored file. CODE (s10/s11/s12): the whole-tree `npm run check && npm test` + confirm each negative control fires its targeted code (gate and commit kept SEPARATE — #3a).

_code_design._note: summary; the load-bearing detail (signatures, regexes, NC tokens, the reader fix) is in the handoff to planning-harden.
_code_design.improvement_5_s10: SOURCE-SCAN, not a static registry. Reason: a component's custom states are added imperatively (button.ts: `requestAnimationFrame(() => this.internals.states?.add('ready'))`, optional-chained, behind rAF) and its slots live only in the CSS (`:has([slot=leading])`) — neither is a statically-inspectable class field, so the truth lives in the .ts/.css source. New: `compareDescriptorToSource(desc, {ts, css}) -> SourceDriftFailure[]` with SOURCE_DRIFT_CODES ['STATE_UNDOCUMENTED','STATE_UNUSED','SLOT_UNDOCUMENTED']; extractors collectUsedStates/collectStyledSlots/collectStyledRoles (regexes verified); a `scalarSeq` helper + a one-line addField fix so bare-scalar sequence items (customStates `- ready`) keep their value (today they parse to an EMPTY map — the value is dropped, and the s8 test only checks presence so the gap is invisible). NO button.ts change.
_code_design.improvement_4_s12: a STATIC dead-name guard (vitest probe in the components package — zero shared-file edits; reuses parseDescriptor + collectStyledRoles by relative import, scans site/ via node:fs). Canonical slot vocab = union of all {name}.md `slots[].name`; canonical role vocab = union of collectStyledRoles({name}.css). Scans both spellings (`slot="x"` AND `setAttribute('slot','x')`). Anti-vacuous + a synthetic dead-name NC. Chose a static lint over a render smoke: the failure is a stale NAME (static), jsdom can't see the :has() grid, and a browser smoke is specimen-coupled.

_open_decisions.improvement_6_review_step_vs_tooling: RECOMMEND a documented review step (+ the coverage_check scope note), NOT a tooling gate. Matching an ADR Decision (prose) to a decomp action is semantic, not true/false — a fuzzy gate would violate the determinism->code placement rule and be brittle/vacuous. (An optional weak lint: every ADR ID the decomp cites has >=1 action referencing it; noted, not recommended.)
_open_decisions.improvement_4_guard_kind: RECOMMEND a static lint/test (the dead-name guard above), NOT a site render smoke — specified in s12.
_open_decisions.improvement_4_guard_home: RECOMMEND Option 1 — the guard lives in the components package (zero shared-file edits, fully disjoint, reuses the extractors directly). Alternative Option 4 — under site/ with vitest `include` += 'site/**/*.test.ts' (correct dependency direction, but one shared edit -> a serial integration slice). The reverse-coupling (a package test reading site/) is a dev-only gate, mirroring the existing button-descriptor.test.ts fs-read precedent.

_coverage_prediction.expected_stdout: == coverage: 17 nodes . 21 actions . 21 hosts == / [OK] coverage clean — both planes cross-check
_coverage_prediction.rationale: 17 nodes (5 non-leaf groups + 12 leaf slices), 21 actions, 21 hosts. Every action is hosted exactly once (no UNHOSTED), every hosts edge resolves to a real id (no DANGLING), all ids unique (no DUP-ID), and every leaf slice hosts >=1 action (no UNJUSTIFIED-LEAF under --strict). Non-leaf groups carry leaf:false so they are exempt.
